items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit ...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit system creates a formalisation cascade where firms demand invoices from suppliers & this pulls the entire supply chain slowly into the formal economy


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganisation at the global scale: only the most productive firms set up GCCs, trading off lower wages against higher coordination costs of remote work.


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21294
Services Across Borders: How Firms Organize Remote Work at Scale
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/2gIf50YuL4m
#CEPR_ITRE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: 45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enfo...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enforcement but because every buyer demands an invoice from the supplier behind it.
Formalisation cascading begins with large firms and grows upstream


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21163
Self-Enforcing Tax Design and Supply Chain Formalization: Evidence from India’s GST Reform
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/5QK550YfcL1
#CEPR_PE #EconTwitter

items.title: My two-cents on The Odyssey: I came away underwhelmed. Visually, it is spectacular, with some incredible set pieces. The most impressive are Circe tur...
items.description_html:
My two-cents on The Odyssey: I came away underwhelmed.

Visually, it is spectacular, with some incredible set pieces. The most impressive are Circe turning the men into pigs (I would watch a spinoff just about Circe), the Cyclops Polyphemus in his cave, Scylla striking from the rocks, and the towering Laestrygonians.

But the film has no soul. And one important part of The Odyssey is about a man searching for his soul, working out who he really is after victory and glory. It felt like Nolan seems to have absorbed too much from recent superhero movies—the ones that hand you great action, visuals, and set pieces but leave little emotional residue behind once you walk out.

What I liked: The film is visually incredible. The sound design is among the best I have experienced in one of these big-experience movies, and the production design, the cinematography, the use of IMAX film, and the absence of CGI are all impressive. The war scenes are pretty good (though I don’t know why Agamemnon is wearing a Darth Vader-esque costume). The casting, which has riled up everyone, didn’t bother me. Argos was the best casting in the movie, followed by Pattinson as Antinous.

What baffled me: The real failure is that the gods and demigods are barely there. A Greek epic without the gods talking, interfering, and conspiring makes little sense to me. This is the Hollywood version, stripped of the mysticism and uncertainty at the heart of the story. Athena is reduced to almost nothing. She shakes her head, her eyes brim with tears, and that is about all the film gives her. Hermes never appears. Calypso comes off as a hippie island girl, not a nymph deity.

Once you take the gods and goddesses out, the whole thing goes flat, because now Odysseus reads only as a poor, lost man trying to get home (insert the usual meme about Hollywood trying to bring Matt Damon home). But Odysseus is a proud man, a vain man, who thinks he controls his destiny until the gods put him in his place over and over again. In Nolan’s version, the wrath of each god turns up as another weather event. There are too many stormy scenes. I understand that it is a seafaring journey, but there is far more to it than that.

The biggest disappointment was the music, which was soulless and flat. Losing the Sirens’ song and turning it into a voice-over was a poor choice.

It is definitely worth a watch. It is not Nolan’s best movie. And to truly experience The Odyssey, read the book.

items.title: RT Data For India: New Data Byte: The choice of baseline affects how we see warming, by Juhi Chatterjee One of the best measures to understand warming...
items.description_html:
RT Data For India
New Data Byte: The choice of baseline affects how we see warming, by Juhi Chatterjee

One of the best measures to understand warming is through temperature anomalies. A temperature anomaly is the departure of the temperature for a particular year from a baseline. Positive anomalies indicate warmer than average conditions, while negative anomalies indicate cooler conditions when compared to the baseline.

This baseline or reference period is also referred to as the Long Period Average (LPA), and represents the average climatic conditions over a multi-decadal period of typically 30 years when measuring changes in temperature.

Temperatures today are typically measured against a reference period of 1991-2020, which is the baseline now in use by the India Meteorological Department, the official source of climatological data in India. However, looking at a range of baselines can provide important insights.

Using older baselines, such as 1951-1980, produces larger positive anomalies for present day temperatures with the anomaly estimated to be 0.98°C in 2024. More recent baselines, such as 1991-2020 produce smaller anomaly values for the same year with anomalies reducing to around 0.65°C. An intermediate baseline of 1981-2010 shows warming of a magnitude in between the two at approximately 0.78°C for 2024.

Updating baselines is important for climate monitoring because newer reference periods represent the current climatic conditions experienced by populations better. At the same time, older baselines highlight the cumulative scale of long-term warming. All baselines, however, show a warming trend.

#Climate #Temperature #Heat #India #DataForIndia

items.title: QatarEnergy is reportedly preparing to extend its LNG force majeure till mid-Oct'26. Key takeaways: • Qatar supplies ~20% of global LNG exports, with...
items.description_html:
QatarEnergy is reportedly preparing to extend its LNG force majeure till mid-Oct'26.

Key takeaways:
• Qatar supplies ~20% of global LNG exports, with almost all cargoes passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
• Extended disruption could keep the global LNG market tight especially for Europe & Asia.
• Spot LNG prices may remain elevated as buyers turn to US cargoes
• For India higher LNG import costs could pressure CGDs, industrial gas consumers & domestic gas prices.

Inputs from Yes Securities note

items.title: RT Shereen Bhan: Govt considers changing policy to allow airport operators to own & run airlines per BBG. The Aviation Ministry has not commented on t...
items.description_html:
RT Shereen Bhan
Govt considers changing policy to allow airport operators to own & run airlines per BBG. The Aviation Ministry has not commented on the query sent to them. If this happens, it will pave the way for the two private airport operators Adani & GMR to enter the airline business. For the Adani group, this is the only missing piece in its aviation ecosystem which spans airports, MRO, ground handling among other services. In Dec 2025, just ahead of the launch of the Navi Mumbai airport, I asked Jeet Adani, if they would consider the airline business should the Govt change the rules. He said while they are always opens to opportunities, airlines don’t fit their “capital discipline” and “high margin” profile. Will things change? Watch 👇
@CNBCTV18Live @CNBCTV18News #Aviation #Airline #Adani


CNBC-TV18: ✈️ Govt Weighs Letting Airport Owners Run Airlines: Report
🏗️ Adani, GMR may get to own & run airlines
Current cap stands at 10% for Delhi, Mumbai operators

CNBC-TV18 has reached out to the Aviation Ministry on the report; comment awaited

@ShereenBhan @jeet_adani1 @madeehamedia

items.title: The leveraged FCNR product is a well discussed, but possibly misunderstood a little. An individual that pays tax in his home country will have an issu...
items.description_html:
The leveraged FCNR product is a well discussed, but possibly misunderstood a little.

An individual that pays tax in his home country will have an issue. If you borrow at 5% and get an FCNR at 6% it seems like a spread of 1% that can be leveraged 10x to get 10%. But that's not how tax authorities look at individuals, usually. The income is subject to tax, but the interest outflow is usually not allowed to be netted out from that income - though the US might allow it.

There's a NIIT (Net Investment Income Tax) in the US that applies if your income after deducting interest is higher than about 250K USD or so - but if you're getting 16% it's a fairly small percentage of the extra that doesn't diminish effective returns by much (3.8% of the 16% is 0.6% or so)

There could be a withholding tax on interest received if you borrow/lend outside your country - that is, loan from a gift city bank. FCNR doesn't have a withholding tax on payouts. Even if it did, that doesn't increase the cost of your loan, it just prepays part of the taxes you will owe anyhow on the interest earned. {Edited this part for clarity}

Typical withholding taxes when you PAY interest are held back from the interest paid itself. So if you pay 5% and withholding tax is 10%, you pay 4.5% as interest and 0.5% to your government. So it's not an additional cost.

Given that 6% is attractive (and Indian FCNR goes from 6% to 7.1% also), it's possible that a lot of FCNR deposits simply come from unleveraged players. For those that want the leverage, the tax implication of the countries they are resident in becomes important - so the middle east is the simplest (since there's no tax). The US, singapore, UK and Australia (regions with high Indian NRI presence) will have to get some tax advice before they take leverage, especially if they're getting loans from Gift city or such.

items.title: The fcnr route is definitely interesting for an nri and high returns are only possible because this is restricted to nris only (banks can't do it in t...
items.description_html:
The fcnr route is definitely interesting for an nri and high returns are only possible because this is restricted to nris only (banks can't do it in their treasury, neither can fpis, nor resident indians)

It's a tax free (indian tax) yield of 16% in usd terms. Rbi guarantees the exchange ratio on the principal. Banks get to borrow at 6% or so, and their own foreign branches offer the leverage against the collateral of the deposit (so it's low risk leverage, as a default is fully covered by the deposit)

Since only nris can access it, the 16% return is limited - and for a limited time till sep 30, 2026.

There are small inflows this far but let's see how it evolves.


Neil Borate: Nationalized Banks bring out the 'sasta' FCNR!

Two state owned banks, IDBI Bank and Central Bank of India, have rolled out leveraged FCNR deposit products with a ticket size of just $50,000, well below what most private banks demand for entry. FCNR is a dollar deposit NRIs can

items.title: RT ANI: #WATCH | Mumbai, Maharashtra | CareEdge Ratings Chief Economist Rajani Sinha says, “If you look at the latest data, the trade deficit has wid...
items.description_html:
RT ANI
#WATCH | Mumbai, Maharashtra | CareEdge Ratings Chief Economist Rajani Sinha says, “If you look at the latest data, the trade deficit has widened in June, and it's because of all components. It's not just oil imports which flared up in June, but it's also because of a sharp increase in non-oil imports and also because of a rise in gold imports… I would say that while trade deficit we expect it to remain high, overall we expect CAD (Current Account Deficit) to still remain comfortable… Overall exports have grown by a good 16%. The important point to note is that the very good growth in non-oil imports is a good indication… We are expecting CAD in the range of 0.8 to 1.2% of GDP, which basically means that we are factoring in good growth in goods exports. We expect a healthy momentum in services exports to continue, and also remittances have remained healthy. So broadly, I would say that even with trade widening and trade deficit widening, I don't find it very concerning. CAD is likely to remain in the range of 0.8 to 1.2% of GDP in FY27.”

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗮'𝘀 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗮𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗲?

𝗜𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁? 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗮𝗹𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗽𝗼𝗼𝗹? 𝗢𝗿 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝘂𝗰𝗵 𝗯𝗶𝗴𝗴𝗲𝗿?

In this insightful exchange, @RevatiKasture asks a fundamental question about India's growth opportunity, and @rnivruti Rai explains why India's strength lies beyond consumption, it's increasingly becoming a critical part of global supply chains, offering not just demand, but the building blocks of manufacturing itself.

From components to capabilities, India's value proposition is evolving.

Watch this powerful perspective on India's next growth chapter.

items.title: CareEdge Ratings was proud to be the Knowledge Partner at the @ASSOCHAM4India 17th Mutual Fund Summit, held on July 3, 2026, in Delhi, where industry ...
items.description_html:
CareEdge Ratings was proud to be the Knowledge Partner at the @ASSOCHAM4India 17th Mutual Fund Summit, held on July 3, 2026, in Delhi, where industry leaders came together to discuss the evolving landscape of India's asset management ecosystem.

The summit was graced by the Chief Guest, Shri Amarjeet Singh, Whole Time Director, Securities and Exchange Board of India (@SEBI_India).

A key highlight of the summit was the release of our knowledge report, “Mutual Funds – Capitalising on India's Ascent for Viksit Bharat”, offering insights into the sector's growth trajectory and its role in supporting India's long-term economic aspirations.

Our experts played an active role in driving meaningful conversations:

Sanjay Agarwal, Senior Director at CareEdge Ratings, delivered a Special Address and moderated an engaging session on "Investment and Distribution Strategies: The Road Ahead in a Dynamic Market," exploring the evolving landscape of investor engagement and distribution.

Saurabh Bhalerao, Director, CareEdge Ratings, participated as a panellist in "Leveraging Technology & FinTech Disruption: Innovation, Opportunities & Challenges, and Investor Education," sharing perspectives on how technology is reshaping the mutual fund ecosystem.

#MutualFunds #AssetManagement #InvestmentStrategy #FinTech #InvestorEducation #CapitalMarkets #ViksitBharat #IndiaGrowthStory #ThoughtLeadership #CareEdgeRatings #BFSI #EconomicGrowth

𝗪𝗵𝗲𝗻 𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝗙𝗗𝗜 𝗳𝗹𝗼𝘄𝘀 𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗿 𝘁𝗼 𝗯𝗲 𝗳𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴, 𝗱𝗼𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝘄𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲, 𝗼𝗿 𝗶𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗮 𝗯𝗶𝗴𝗴𝗲𝗿 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗯𝗲𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗮?

In this insightful conversation, Revati Kasture, ED, CareEdge Ratings and CEO, CareEdge Global, raises a critical question on declining net FDI, while Nivruti Rai, MD and CEO, Invest India, explains why rising reinvestments and growing outward investments actually paint a much stronger picture of India's investment landscape.

Sometimes, the headline number doesn't tell the whole story.

Watch this thought-provoking exchange on India’s FDI story.

Watch Now: https://youtu.be/xCGjJHOG-ww

items.title: North Sea and Mediterranean physical oil markets have surged today after European refiners rushed to offset the loss of short-haul barrels from Kazakh...
items.description_html:
North Sea and Mediterranean physical oil markets have surged today after European refiners rushed to offset the loss of short-haul barrels from Kazakhstan. Physical differentials have risen to $3-$6 per barrel.


Javier Blas: More than the Middle East, for European crude this is the big news of the day: Kazakhstan made a “temporary reduction” of its oil production as the CPC pipeline system stops crude intake (following Ukrainian attacks on the terminal in the Black Sea).

items.title: The Chinese-flagged oil tanker has now emerged from the Bab el-Mandeb strait with a load of 2 million barrels of Saudi oil. Another Chinese tanker is ...
items.description_html:
The Chinese-flagged oil tanker has now emerged from the Bab el-Mandeb strait with a load of 2 million barrels of Saudi oil. Another Chinese tanker is about to emerge too. For now (emphasis on “for now), the Houthis are enforcing their blockade differently depending on the flag.


Javier Blas: At least one of the oil tankers that yesterday U-turned in the Red Sea has performed a 2nd U-turn, heading again toward the Bab el-Mandeb controlled by the Houthis.

The vessel, carrying Saudi oil, is signalling "CHINA CREW & OWNER." At current speed, she will cross the strait in

items.title: RT Jorge Liboreiro: Important: In exchange for lifting its veto, Greece has secured an exemption to continue shipping Russian LNG to non-EU clients af...
items.description_html:
RT Jorge Liboreiro
Important: In exchange for lifting its veto, Greece has secured an exemption to continue shipping Russian LNG to non-EU clients after January 2027.

It will apply to contracts signed before February 2022 and will be reviewed every year.


Jorge Liboreiro: It's done: after several failed attempts, ambassadors have reached a deal on a new package of sanctions against Russia. Greece has lifted its veto.

More details soon.

items.title: With electricity demand growing so fast, even the spectacular growth in PV (and wind, to a lesser extent) is insufficient to meet the annual increase ...
items.description_html:
With electricity demand growing so fast, even the spectacular growth in PV (and wind, to a lesser extent) is insufficient to meet the annual increase in consumption, let alone make a dent on baseload fossil fuel output. As a result, coal is still called on to fill the gap.


Javier Blas: The @IEA has released its mid-year update for electricity markets. The highlight is the strong growth in demand (3.6% in 2026, from 3% in 2025). Don't blame just data centers / AI, the growth is broad-based, with industrial expansion, EVs, AC and heat pumps all playing a role.

items.description_html:
Services exports recorded strong momentum in December, rising 21.9% MoM and 13.3% YoY, the first instance of double-digit annual growth in three months.

Services imports increased 7.4% YoY, following a contraction in November (-2.04%), contributing to the net expansion.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:
In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY.

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: December’s pickup followed a 7.2% expansion in November, a clear improvement over the 3.7% growth a year earlier. The acceleration was supported by s...
items.description_html: December’s pickup followed a 7.2% expansion in November, a clear improvement over the 3.7% growth a year earlier. The acceleration was supported by strength in manufacturing, mining, and electricity. Consumer durables and infrastructure goods also posted double-digit gains.

items.title: BREAKING: The median sale price of existing US homes increased +2.2% YoY in June to a record $408,776. San Francisco led the increase at +9.2% YoY, fo...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The median sale price of existing US homes increased +2.2% YoY in June to a record $408,776.

San Francisco led the increase at +9.2% YoY, followed by Pittsburgh at +9.1%, and West Palm Beach at +8.6%, with luxury purchases driving much of the gain.

San Francisco and West Palm Beach also recorded the largest increases in closed home sales, rising +23.8% and +23.1% respectively, pointing to a sharp pickup in market activity.

This comes as homebuying demand strengthened heading into the summer, with existing home sales rising +4.2% YoY, to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of ~4.4 million in June, the highest since November 2022.

At the same time, new listings fell -0.8% MoM, to 377,000, their lowest level since December.

US housing affordability is deteriorating again.

items.title: Institutional demand for Japanese government bonds surging: Japanese insurers bought +$3.9 billion of 10+ year Japanese government bonds (JGBs) in Jun...
items.description_html:
Institutional demand for Japanese government bonds surging:

Japanese insurers bought +$3.9 billion of 10+ year Japanese government bonds (JGBs) in June, the largest monthly purchase in 3 years.

This marks a sharp reversal from -$1.2 billion sold in the prior month.

This is also the 2nd consecutive monthly purchase over the last 11 months.

Insurers are one of the biggest domestic buyers of long-term JGBs, and their return could provide an important source of support for the market.

The shift comes as authorities move to strengthen domestic demand, including a proposal to allow government bonds in a tax-free investment program for individuals, and encourage large pension funds to increase allocations to Japanese assets.

Japan’s bond market may finally be finding a material source of demand.

items.title: BREAKING: US job postings on Indeed fell -3.5% YoY in the week ending July 10th, to near the lowest this year. Postings have declined -37.2% since Apr...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US job postings on Indeed fell -3.5% YoY in the week ending July 10th, to near the lowest this year.

Postings have declined -37.2% since April 2022 and are now down to February 2021 levels.

The number of available vacancies in the US is now only +1.0% above pre-pandemic levels recorded in February 2020.

Furthermore, new job postings on Indeed dropped -10.9% YoY, to the lowest since early January, continuing their over 4-year decline.

As a result, new job postings are now -3.5% below February 2020 levels.

This all suggests a further deterioration in official BLS job openings data for June and July.

The US labor market is losing momentum.

items.title: Chinese investors are buying the dip: China's largest tech fund, the Star 50 ETF, attracted a record +$2.0 billion in inflows on Monday, its largest d...
items.description_html:
Chinese investors are buying the dip:

China's largest tech fund, the Star 50 ETF, attracted a record +$2.0 billion in inflows on Monday, its largest daily intake on record.

This is more than twice the previous record set in December 2020.

As a result, the Star 50 index surged +11% on Tuesday, its biggest daily gain in nearly 2 years.

This follows a -17% decline last week as investors unwound leveraged positions by the largest amount since the 2015-2016 equity market crash.

China’s "National Team" ETFs have attracted +$10.0 billion in inflows over the last 3 trading sessions, the largest intake since April 2025.

Meanwhile, at least 5 of China's largest insurers have pledged to boost stock market investments, with China Life alone buying over +$1.5 billion of stocks and funds.

Chinese investors remain eager to buy dips.

items.title: Global investors have rarely been this bullish: Bank of America's Bull & Bear Indicator is up to 9.6 points, the highest since December 2020. The gaug...
items.description_html:
Global investors have rarely been this bullish:

Bank of America's Bull & Bear Indicator is up to 9.6 points, the highest since December 2020.

The gauge measures equity and bond fund flows, hedge fund and fund manager equity positioning, credit market conditions, and market breadth.

This metric is now at its 3rd-highest level in 24 years, indicating an extremely positive market sentiment.

This comes as 5 out of 6 indicator components show bullish and very bullish readings, with only global stock market breadth at neutral.

The most recent increase in the gauge has been driven by fund manager cash allocation, which has fallen -0.5 percentage points month-over-month, to 3.6%, near the lowest in 13 years.

Investor appetite for stocks has rarely ever been stronger.

items.title: Trump is back again with tariffs, and pharma stocks got hit today. Here's what I think Trump has announced that imported generic drugs into the US wil...
items.description_html:
Trump is back again with tariffs, and pharma stocks got hit today.

Here's what I think

Trump has announced that imported generic drugs into the US will continue to have 0% tariff for the first two years (from Aug 1), then 100% in the third year and 200% thereafter.

But here's what most people are missing:

-This applies only to imported generics.
-Many Indian pharma companies already make medicines in the US. For example, Sun Pharma has 4 manufacturing plants in the US(also recently acquired Organon-US pharma), and Cipla has 5 plants in the US.
-Also, the higher tariffs will only start from 2028, so the actual impact on earnings is likely from 2029. Companies have enough time to adjust.

The market is reacting much faster than the actual impact. I don't think this changes much for companies with US manufacturing exposure, and CDMOs should see limited impact as they work with US innovators.

items.title: Whats the flipside of this? US still needs generic medicines and companies will pass on costs to the consumers anyway. So the local consumers would ha...
items.description_html: Whats the flipside of this? US still needs generic medicines and companies will pass on costs to the consumers anyway. So the local consumers would have to buy at inflated rates. Also I doubt this will go into effect after 2 years. Its a net loss to US

#MacroSignal India's affluent economy has been powered by IT and BFSI for nearly two decades. What happens when those engines begin to slow?

IT wage growth has halved from 13% to 6%. Financial services from 11% to 5%.
The top-5 IT companies have added virtually no net jobs in three years.
Top private banks are now cutting headcount. And lenders have begun flagging early stress among IT borrowers.

Our latest #MacroSignal connects the dots behind what could be the first cracks in India's affluent consumption story and why markets should pay attention @_RituSingh @CNBCTV18News @CNBCTV18Live #Jobs #Tech #Banks #India


Ritu Singh: IT & banking salaries built India's affluent consumer- first car, first SIP, first credit card. That engine's losing steam.

NSE-500 wage growth has halved in 2 yrs, mostly in these 2 sectors, & bankers like @JairamSridharan are flagging early stress.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: IndusInd BK - Good Qtr.. but stk rallied off late in anticipation & trades at a FY28E P/BV of 1.2x Jefferies on IndusInd Bk Buy, TP Ra...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
IndusInd BK - Good Qtr.. but stk rallied off late in anticipation & trades at a FY28E P/BV of 1.2x

Jefferies on IndusInd Bk
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1250
Q1 show progress on improving trends in growth, asset quality, and core ROA.
Profit of Rs10bn was ahead of ests, and even core trends were better, aided by lower opex and credit costs
Expect improvements to continue over the next 2-3 years as bank leverages new team buildup
Boost est (from a low base) & see ROA of 1.1% by FY29
Val at 1.3x FY27 adj PB looks fair
Visibility on c.1.5% ROA will be key to a rerating.

CLSA on IndusInd Bk
Downgrade to U-P from hold, TP Raised to Rs 925
Mixed 1QFY27
Adjusted for one-offs, NII was in line with estimates while lower Opex & credit costs led to core PBT of Rs10.6bn vs estimate of Rs8.5bn
What was good during the quarter? Retail deposit growth picked up, albeit driven by retail TD.
Opex was under control and net slippage improved off a high base driven solely by MFI (net slippage ratio down 660bps QoQ).
What was not so good? Loan growth of 3.6% QoQ was driven solely by corporate loans.
Core NIM was marginally down while fee income remained muted.
Management continues to guide for industry-level loan growth in FY27 with an exit ROA of 1%.
Stock has rallied 17% over past month, which believe is unwarranted

CITI on IndusInd Bk
Sell, TP raised to Rs 990
Core RoA expanded to 0.63% (vs. 0.45% QoQ), surpassing CitiE estimates, driven by opex efficiency and credit cost moderation.
Revenue held firm — core NIMs declined 4bps QoQ, offset by an equivalent 4bps fee/assets uptick. Rs2.84bn of interest on IT refund lifted reported RoA to 0.78%.
Loan growth of 3% was overwhelmingly wholesale-driven (+11% QoQ), with Retail (flat) and SME (-2%) continuing to lag.
Mgmt. identified Q1 as an inflection point, signalling completion of balance sheet recalibration
Path to 1% RoA by FY27-end is projected to be 60% PPOP-driven & 40% credit cost-driven.
Incorporating IT refund interest income, lower opex, & credit cost improvement, revise FY27E/FY28E earnings upward by 11%/5% (FY29E introduced).

JPM on IndusInd Bk
UW, TP raised to Rs 820
1QFY27 PAT of Rs10.4bn rose by 72% y/y, 75% q/q & came in 59% higher vs JPMe with reported NII/PPoP beating JPMe by 6%/19%, respectively.
Core NII, excluding Rs2.84bn of interest on tax refund, rose by 1% q/q and came in line with JPMe.
Core NIMs declined by 4bps q/q to 3.35% on loan mix change (higher wholesale, lower VF) & higher RIDF.
AQ continues to improve with Gross slippages declining by 9% q/q to Rs 16.6bn despite the seasonal rise in vehicle finance slippages
IIB retained its target of growing in line with market in FY27 with 1% exit RoA with operating leverage and lower credit costs being key enablers
IIB stock is up 23% in last 3M (vs -2% NIFTY) & trades at a FY28E P/BV of 1.2x, which believe already discounts delivery of a 1% RoA, thus making risk-reward unfavorable
@CNBCTV18Live @latha_venkatesh @_RituSingh

items.title: 🚨 C2C’s auditor has issued a qualified opinion and flagged multiple serious financial and compliance concerns: 1️⃣ Liquidity & Going Concern Ris...
items.description_html:
🚨 C2C’s auditor has issued a qualified opinion and flagged multiple serious financial and compliance concerns:

1️⃣ Liquidity & Going Concern Risk:
The company is facing a liquidity crisis, with statutory dues unpaid due to lack of funds. Recovery from trade receivables remains uncertain, while operations are heavily dependent on short-term promoter funding. The auditor has raised doubt over C2C’s ability to continue as a going concern. Liquidation Risk

2️⃣ CSR Payments Not Cleared:
CSR expenses were recognised through cheques, creating a book overdraft. However, the cheques were not presented for payment. The auditor noted that the CSR obligation is discharged only when the payment is actually cleared.

3️⃣ Statutory Dues Pending:
Undisputed statutory payments remain unpaid. No provision has been made for possible interest or penalties. Delays in filing statutory returns and weak internal controls were also highlighted.

4️⃣ Labour Code Impact Not Assessed:
The company has not quantified the potential impact of new labour regulations on gratuity, provident fund, wages and other employee benefits.

5️⃣ FEMA Compliance Issues:
Overseas receivables remain unrealised beyond RBI timelines. Applications for regularisation are pending, SOFTEX registration is yet to be approved, and possible penalties have not been provided for.

items.description_html:
Recode Studios Acquires 51% Stake in Premium Beauty Brand Aflairza

Recode Studios is strengthening its Beauty & Personal Care (BPC) portfolio with the acquisition of a controlling stake in premium beauty brand Aflairza.

- The brand has demonstrated superior value realization, reflecting its premium positioning

- Acquisition aligns with Recode's strategy to expand its presence in the high-growth Beauty & Personal Care category

- Recode plans to leverage its product development capabilities to accelerate Aflairza's growth

- Company will also utilize its distribution network and operational expertise to scale the brand

- Management aims to strengthen Aflairza's market presence across India

- Founders will continue to partner with Recode in the next phase of expansion

- Management's long-term vision is to build one of India's leading premium beauty businesses

It gives Recode a stronger presence in the premium beauty segment, where higher pricing power and better margins can create long-term value. Leveraging Recode's distribution, product development and execution capabilities could significantly accelerate Aflairza's scale over the coming years.

Recode will initially acquire a 33% stake in Phase I for an aggregate consideration of ₹8.32 Crore, with equal allocation between promoter share purchase and fresh capital into the company.

~ Margins expanded from 12% to 23%
~ Works on an asset-light model with a focus

items.title: Creative Newtech has done an interesting acquisition, transforming from a distributor to a manufacturer of an integrated surveillance technology platf...
items.description_html:
Creative Newtech has done an interesting acquisition, transforming from a distributor to a manufacturer of an integrated surveillance technology platform.

Creative Newtech has approved the acquisition of 100% of Infinova (India) for up to US$4 million, marking a strategic shift beyond distribution into surveillance technology manufacturing.

The acquisition brings Infinova's India operations, brand rights, technical expertise, experienced workforce, and local assembly & manufacturing facility, providing Creative Newtech with immediate execution capabilities.

More importantly, the deal lays the foundation for a 'Make in India' surveillance platform, enabling the company to build proprietary products, strengthen after-sales support, and reduce dependence on third-party distribution.

The transaction also accelerates Creative's transition from a distribution-led business to an integrated, technology-led surveillance solutions provider, giving it greater control over manufacturing, product ownership, and margins.

The company holds all the relevant licenses from Gem, BIS, and STQC, enabling it to access the government portal. STQC shifts 22% market share from Chinese to Indian brands

This is more than an acquisition—it is a business model transformation, positioning Creative Newtech to move up the value chain from distributing surveillance products to designing, assembling, and manufacturing them under a 'Make in India' strategy.

Creative NewTech trades at 18 PE vs Prizor Vtech 42PE vs Aditya Infotech 110PE

A unique re-rating candidate driven by undervaluation, business transformation, and sector tailwinds

items.description_html:

items.title: RT Surenddra verma: Great Punjabi Lala Jagat Narain. He spent years in prison for opposing both British rule and the Emergency. He built Punjab Kesari...
items.description_html:
RT Surenddra verma
Great Punjabi Lala Jagat Narain.
He spent years in prison for opposing both British rule and the Emergency. He built Punjab Kesari into one of Punjab's most widely read newspapers and refused to bow before terror.

He stood up to Bhindranwale despite repeated threats. He was assassinated for his courage. The sacrifices did not end there-his son, Ramesh Chander, was also killed, along with 52 members of the Punjab Kesari family.

The media group lost:
• 2 Editors
• 7 Reporters
• 20 Distribution Agents
• 23 Hawkers
• 2 Workers

Their only "crime" was refusing to surrender to Khalistani intimidation and continuing to print the truth.

Punjab Kesari also raised funds and supported the families of Hindu and Sikh civilians murdered by Khalistani terrorists when the state had largely failed them.

When fear forced distributors to stop carrying the newspaper, KPS Gill ensured copies were distributed through police stations so the voice of truth would not be silenced.

Lala Jagat Narain, the Punjab Kesari family, and KPS Gill remind us that Punjab was saved not only by brave security personnel but also by fearless journalists and ordinary citizens who refused to bow before terror.

Their sacrifice must never be forgotten. 🇮🇳

items.title: RT Reads with Ravi: Umberto Eco, who owned 50,000 books, had this to say about home libraries: “It is foolish to think that you have to read all the ...
items.description_html:
RT Reads with Ravi
Umberto Eco, who owned 50,000 books, had this to say about home libraries:

“It is foolish to think that you have to read all the books you buy, as it is foolish to criticize those who buy more books than they will ever be able to read. It would be like saying that you should use all the cutlery or glasses or screwdrivers or drill bits you bought before buying new ones.

“There are things in life that we need to always have plenty of supplies, even if we will only use a small portion.

“If, for example, we consider books as medicine, we understand that it is good to have many at home rather than a few: when you want to feel better, then you go to the ‘medicine closet’ and choose a book. Not a random one, but the right book for that moment. That’s why you should always have a nutrition choice!

“Those who buy only one book, read only that one and then get rid of it. They simply apply the consumer mentality to books, that is, they consider them a consumer product, a good. Those who love books know that a book is anything but a commodity.”

items.title: RT Sann: His name is Dinesh Thakur. He gave up a safe, high paying job and spent eight years of his life fighting one of the largest drug companies in...
items.description_html:
RT Sann
His name is Dinesh Thakur.

He gave up a safe, high paying job and spent eight years of his life fighting one of the largest drug companies in the country. He did it because he discovered that the medicines millions of people trusted were built on a lie.

He was a chemical engineer who had worked in America and come back to India for a senior role at Ranbaxy, then the biggest generic medicine maker in the country. It was a proud Indian company whose cheap medicines were sold across the world.

Not long after he joined, he was asked to look into the quality of the company's drugs. What he found frightened him.

The company was faking its test results. Medicines were being sold without being properly tested for whether they were safe, or whether they even worked. The data handed to regulators was simply invented.

These were not luxury goods. These were the tablets that sick people swallowed every day, trusting that they would heal them.

Thakur went to his own bosses with what he had found. Nothing changed. So he made a choice that cost him everything. He resigned, and he reported the company to the American drug regulators.

Then began eight long years. He handed over evidence, sat through investigation after investigation, and lived under the shadow of taking on a giant that had every reason to crush him.

In 2013, it ended. Ranbaxy admitted its guilt in an American court, pleaded guilty to seven serious crimes, and was made to pay one of the largest fines ever handed to a generic drug maker, five hundred million dollars.

Because one man refused to look away, the way the world checks the safety of its medicines was changed for good.

He had a comfortable life and a good salary. He gave it all up so that the medicine in a stranger's hand could be trusted.

items.title: RT The Seeker: It's a shame no major film has been made about Biju Patnaik. He lived a life that sounds almost fictional: In 1938, he flew his entire ...
items.description_html:
RT The Seeker
It's a shame no major film has been made about Biju Patnaik. He lived a life that sounds almost fictional:

In 1938, he flew his entire wedding procession by aircraft to Rawalpindi for his marriage to Gyanwati Sethi (who became the first Indian woman to hold a commercial pilot’s license, and the couple later flew daring missions together.)

While serving in the Royal Indian Air Force (including as head of Air Transport Command), he secretly ferried freedom fighters like Jayaprakash Narayan, Ram Manohar Lohia, and Aruna Asaf Ali to their hideouts. He also airdropped 'Quit India' leaflets to Indian troops while evacuating British families.

The British once jailed him for transporting nationalist leaders and distributing anti-British literature but also honored him for bravery in evacuating civilians from Japanese-occupied Burma.

During WWII, he flew daring missions delivering arms and supplies to Soviet forces in Stalingrad, and over the treacherous 'Hump' route to China in
support of Chinese Nationalist forces against the Japanese.

In July 1947, he and his wife flew a Dakota into Indonesia, landed on an improvised airstrip while evading Dutch anti-aircraft fire, rescued Prime Minister Sutan Sjahrir, refuelled using abandoned Japanese fuel, and flew him safely to India via Singapore.

A couple of months later, during the Pakistani invasion of Kashmir, he airlifted one of the first Indian troops into Srinagar.

The same year he founded Kalinga Airlines, one of India's earliest private airlines.

As Odisha CM post-1962 India-China war, he secretly visited CIA headquarters at Langley to forge a covert India-US partnership against China, which led to the Aviation Research Centre (ARC) at Charbatia, which supported U-2 recon missions over Tibet.

He was also a legendary administrator and statesman, laying much of the foundation of Odisha's industrial empire (mines, ports, heavy industries etc).

When he passed away, Indonesia observed 7 days of state mourning. Russia observed 1 day. He remains the only person in Indian history whose body was draped in the national flags of 3 nations - India, Indonesia, and Russia.

Few public figures have left such a footprint.

His extraordinary life needs to be brought to the big screen.


Shiv Aroor: What event in Indian history/recent current affairs do you want to see a film made on?

items.title: RT lordmicky 🇵🇹: Arthur Conan Doyle, creator of Sherlock Holmes, once traveled to Switzerland. When he arrived, he took a taxi and the driver to...
items.description_html:
RT lordmicky 🇵🇹
Arthur Conan Doyle, creator of Sherlock Holmes, once traveled to Switzerland. When he arrived, he took a taxi and the driver told him he wouldn't charge him if he autographed a book.

Conan Doyle, surprised, asked the taxi driver how he knew he was a writer, and the taxi driver replied: "Easy, your shoes are covered in dust that isn't from here. From the pattern of your shoes, I see that they are English shoes. So it's English dust. You have an ink stain on your toes, so you are a writer, a British writer."

Shocked, Conan Doyle replied: "You are smarter than Sherlock Holmes."

"Yes sir, and your suitcases clearly have Arthur Conan Doyle written on them."

items.title: RT Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Today marks 250 years since a small group of newspaper editors, farmers, and soldiers signed a document declaring our n...
items.description_html:
RT Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani
Today marks 250 years since a small group of newspaper editors, farmers, and soldiers signed a document declaring our nation’s independence — a truth that feels self-evident now but was revolutionary then.

What a privilege us Americans have, to live in a nation that every one of its inhabitants can shape. What an honor us New Yorkers have, to look out over our city’s waters from the shores where so many Americans bravely entered their country for the first time.

Today and all days, let us remember that patriotism is not pretending our nation is without flaws. Patriotism is every act of righteous dissent — because loving our country means fighting for the best version of it.

Happy Fourth of July, New York City.

GCC's allocate 110 - 130 sqft per person of office space

Traditional Indian IT companies typically pack workspaces tighter at 80 to 110 sq ft per employee

Good for the industry!

items.title: EDUCATION IN INDIA 11-13 suicides of NEET aspirants reported since cancellation of May 3 examination and subsequent re-test. Education Minister accept...
items.description_html:
EDUCATION IN INDIA
11-13 suicides of NEET aspirants reported since cancellation of May 3 examination and subsequent re-test.
Education Minister accepted responsibility for systemic failure, described those responsible as "protectors turned predators”.
CBI has arrested at least 13 people in connection with investigation.
Looking back: India recorded 13,892 student suicides in 2023 — highest in at least a decade, about 8% of all suicides.
Roughly 38 a day. One every 38 minutes. The count is about 72% higher than in 2014.
This can't be pinned on exam pressure alone but it demands a hard look at academic stress, family expectation and access to mental-health care.
For support, reach out to: Tele-MANAS (Govt of India), 24/7: 14416 / 1-800-891-4416.

items.title: EDUCATION IN INDIA About 13.36 lakh Indian students were studying abroad in 2024 — nearly double the 6.75 lakh of 2019. Top destinations: Canada, the...
items.description_html:
EDUCATION IN INDIA
About 13.36 lakh Indian students were studying abroad in 2024 — nearly double the 6.75 lakh of 2019.
Top destinations: Canada, the US, the UK.
India is now the No. 1 source of international students to the US, ahead of China.
NITI Aayog's own flag: India sends roughly 25 students abroad for every one it attracts ("Internationalisation of Higher Education in India," 2025)

items.title: Here's where India stands in global stacking order when it comes to EDUCATION: # India accounts for nearly 18% of humanity and runs one of the world's...
items.description_html:
Here's where India stands in global stacking order when it comes to EDUCATION:
# India accounts for nearly 18% of humanity and runs one of the world's largest higher-education systems.
Yet, no Indian university sits in the global top 100.
# QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026): India's best is IIT Delhi, at 118.
# Times Higher Education World University Rankings 2026: India's best is Indian Institute of Science (IISc) in Bangalore, known as Tata Institute, in the 201–250 band.
What more do we need to say what are we doing for our youth and education?

items.title: RT Dharmendra Pradhan: LoP Shri @RahulGandhi and @INCIndia continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption durin...
items.description_html:
RT Dharmendra Pradhan
LoP Shri @RahulGandhi and @INCIndia continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.

Shri @RahulGandhi and @INCIndia chose to stage a dharna outside the Hon'ble Prime Minister's residence , causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols.

Even after Government conveyed its readiness for a comprehensive discussion, the Congress chose political spectacle over democratic debate. Their objective was never solutions for students, it was disruption for political headlines

For Rahul Gandhi, this is not about students. This is about manufacturing confrontation after every genuine avenue for discussion has been opened.

Our Government remains 100% committed to discussing NEET and addressing every genuine concern of our youth on the floor of the House. The students of India deserve far better than being treated as props in a political campaign. They deserve certainty over chaos, solutions over slogans and responsibility over disruption.

We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability. That is exactly what our government remains committed to delivering.

items.title: As the "cockroach" ‌movement continues, let's look at the numbers behind students' anger. India's education-spend down from 2.9% in FY20 to 2.7% of G...
items.description_html:
As the "cockroach" ‌movement continues, let's look at the numbers behind students' anger.
India's education-spend down from 2.9% in FY20 to 2.7% of GDP in FY26 against 6% target set by National Education Policy 2020. It trails lower-middle-income country average of 3.4% & South Asia regional average of 2.9%.
Health spend at 1.8% in FY26, far lower than 2.5% of GDP target set at National Health Policy 2017.
India's per-capita public health spending is among the world's lowest — less than Bhutan & Sri Lanka & far behind other BRICS nations.
India ranks 130 out of 193 countries on the UN Human Development Index which combines health, education & income. India sits in the "medium human development" category with a value of 0.685. China (78th), Sri Lanka (89th) and Bhutan (125th) rank above India; Bangladesh is tied at 130th.
Ref: UNDP's Human Development Report 2025 ("A Matter of Choice: People and Possibilities in the Age of AI"), based on 2023 data.

items.description_html:
PhonePe has dominated India's digital payments market through UPI for years.

The bigger question has always been whether it can build a meaningful business beyond payments to monetise its over 200 million active users.

It has experimented with multiple businesses across financial services and commerce over the last 3-4 years.

Lending, much like for already-listed rival Paytm, is now emerging as the answer.

Details 👇


Pratik Bhakta: 🚨🚨PhonePe is betting big on credit as it looks to grow, and more importantly, diversify its revenues ahead of an IPO.

Sources familiar with the matter told us that PhonePe has scaled up credit disbursals to more than Rs 2,000 crore a month and scaling very fast.

More than a decade ago when I had started covering fintech, the sector was in its infancy.

Today it spans listed companies, unicorns, digital-first challengers and traditional financial institutions that have transformed into tech-led businesses

📢 Super pumped to welcome Pratik Bhakta (@PratikBhakta_89) to The Arc.

Over the last decade, Pratik has established himself as one of the sharpest reporters covering India's fintech ecosystem. More importantly, he has developed a deep understanding of the policy and regulatory shifts driving the sector.

Fintech is entering a new phase shaped by IPOs, AI, DPI besides all the regulatory change.

Pratik will help our readers make sense of where the industry is headed through sharper analysis, deeper reporting and original insights.

Pratik joins us after more than three years as Deputy Editor at ETTech, part of The Economic Times.

This is also a reunion. We've worked together before, and excited to be collaborating with him again.

items.title: RT Pratik Bhakta: 💰The AI boom is also opening up a major oppprtunity for global payment companies. In the USA, payment giant Stripe is riding the ...
items.description_html:
RT Pratik Bhakta
💰The AI boom is also opening up a major oppprtunity for global payment companies.

In the USA, payment giant Stripe is riding the AI boom with its revenue growing fastest since 2021.

On July 22, The Information citing sources reported that Stripe reported $6.8bn in revenue in 2025. Free cash flow of 3.2bn up 52%.

In 2024 Stripe had reported revenues of around $5bn.

That's massive.

No wonder Stripe wants to acquire PayPal in parthership with Advent.

Makes you wonder how frontier technology firms can also take other tech companies to grow alongside.

If the Indian AI firms Sarvam, Emergent start showing massive growth, imagine the next growth opportunity for India's digital payment startups Razorpay, PayU, Cashfree, Paytm.

that's kinda crazy to think about.

items.description_html:
Eternal's new businesses, which primarily includes Bistro (10 mins food delivery service) and Nugget, had adjusted revenue of Rs 95 crore in April-June 2026 as compared to Rs 4 crore last year.

But adjusted EBITDA loss also doubled from Rs 45 crore to Rs 94 crore.

The loss was "largely driven by R&D investments in Nugget as we scale our AI product capabilities," according to Eternal CFO Akshant Goyal.


Madhav Chanchani: Nugget, the AI-powered customer support platform that Eternal launched in Feb last year, had revenues of Rs 7.2 crore in FY26.

It is being transferred into a subsidiary.

- The personal guarantees Jain had provided over the years for corporate loans will also be released.

items.title: RT Adam Posen: Really important new discussion of added tariffs on Canada and of Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930 Must read @PIIE illuminating
items.description_html:
RT Adam Posen
Really important new discussion of added tariffs on Canada and of Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930
Must read @PIIE illuminating


Peterson Institute: ⁉️What is Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930, the newest tariff authority the Trump administration is using to impose 50% tariffs on Canada?
➡️Section 338 has not been invoked in 100 years & requires strong proof of discrimination against American goods.
